evidence_items.text: Startup loads or initializes a persisted local runtime workspace under `.jeff_runtime`. On a fresh or reset runtime, Jeff initializes `project-1` / `wu-1` with no seeded run history; later startups reload the persisted canonical state and support records already present in that workspace. No daemon, GUI, or background continuation is started.

Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone. It is strongest today at truthful inspection, bounded `/run`, approval-gated continuation, research support, and anti-drift coverage. It is not a GUI, a broad API surface, an autonomous continuation system, or a broad memory product.
After `pip install -e .` the `jeff` console script is also available as a bare command; without the install, use `python -m jeff`.

A local `jeff.runtime.toml` enables the bounded repo-local validation `/run <repo-local-validation-objective>` path and the research commands. Without it, the read-oriented CLI still works, but `/run <repo-local-validation-objective>` and `/research ...` remain unavailable.
```text python -m jeff --help python -m jeff --bootstrap-check python -m jeff --reset-runtime --bootstrap-check ```
```text python -m jeff --command "/help" python -m jeff --command "/project list" python -m jeff --project project-1 --work wu-1 --command "/run list" --json ```
The primary implemented operator surface is the CLI under `jeff.interface`.

- `/run <repo-local-validation-objective>` is the current bounded repo-local validation entry when `jeff.runtime.toml` is loaded. It drives one fixed smoke pytest validation plan with captured command evidence; it is not a general command runner. - `approve` records a bound approval, `revalidate` continues or fails closed, and `reject` terminally blocks that continuation for the current bounded ru

findings.text: Startup initializes or loads a persisted local runtime workspace located under .jeff_runtime
Fresh or reset runtimes initialize project-1 and wu-1 without seeded history, while subsequent startups reload existing canonical state and support records
Startup does not include a GUI, daemon, or background continuation
The system is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone
Access to /run and /research commands during startup requires a local jeff.runtime.toml file

inferences: The presence of jeff.runtime.toml determines whether the system operates as a read-oriented CLI or provides extended validation and research capabilities.
question: Summarize Jeff README startup behavior

source_items.snippet: # Jeff Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone. It is strongest today at truthful inspection, bounded `/run`, approval-gated continuation, research support, and anti-drift coverage. It is not a GUI, a broad API surface, an autonomous continuation system, or a broad memo
source_items.title: README.md

summary: Jeff is a CLI-first persisted-runtime v1 backbone that initializes a local workspace under .jeff_runtime. Upon startup, it either initializes a new project and work unit or reloads the existing canonical state and support records, without starting a GUI, daemon, or background continuation.
uncertainties: No explicit uncertainties identified from the provided evidence.
